title: Config struct: load, validate, and distribute via Arc
description:
Implement config.toml parsing. Struct mirrors spec exactly. Validate on load: secret_key must be exactly 64 hex chars, domain must be a valid hostname (no scheme, no trailing slash), listen must be a valid SocketAddr, paths must be non-empty. Distribute as Arc<Config> via axum State.

Agent workflow: Spawn a subagent to enumerate all config validation edge cases (IPv6 listen addresses, internationalized domain names, paths with spaces).

Test discipline:
- Test with a valid config: parses correctly
- Test missing required field: produces a clear error naming the field
- Test invalid secret_key (wrong length, non-hex): specific error message
- Test invalid domain (contains scheme, port, trailing slash): clear rejection
- Do NOT test by trying to start the server — test the Config::load() function directly with string input

Defensive programming:
- Never log the config struct (it contains secret_key)
- Provide Config::redacted() returning String for logging that masks secret_key
- domain validation: reject if contains '://', reject if starts/ends with '.', reject if contains spaces
acceptance_criteria: Config loads from file and from test string; invalid configs produce error messages naming the invalid field; Arc<Config> flows to all axum handlers; secret_key never appears in logs
